7.reason: Error: When i is 0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory. Also, the condition should check that both sides are valid and of the same type before comparing.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: When i is 0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory. Also, the condition should check that both sides are valid and of the same type before comparing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: This condition does not correctly handle cases where the characters are of different types (e.g., 'a' and '9'), and it also fails to check that both characters are of the same type before comparing. The problem requires expansion only when both characters are of the same type (lowercase, uppercase, or digit) and the second is greater than the first. This condition may incorrectly trigger expansion for mixed types.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Condition should be s[i-1] < s[i+1] (no -1) and missing boundary check for i>0 and i<len-1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Without bounds check (i>0 and i<len-1) may access invalid memory; also condition should be s[i-1] < s[i+1] to allow expansion when left+1==right
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Accessing s[i-1] when i=0 and s[i+1] when s[i] is the last character causes out-of-bounds array access. Boundary checks (i>0 && s[i+1]!='\0') must be added before accessing neighboring characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The condition incorrectly uses '-1', preventing expansion of adjacent characters like 'a-b'. It should be 's[i-1] < s[i+1]'. Also, accessing s[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect: it accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i>0 and i is not at the last character, leading to potential out-of-bounds access. Also, the logic should first verify that both surrounding characters are of the same valid type before comparing their values.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  // This condition is incorrect: it should check that both surrounding characters are of the same type and that s[i-1] < s[i+1], not s[i-1] < s[i+1] - 1. Also, accessing s[i-1] when i=0 leads to undefined behavior (out-of-bounds access).

10.reason: [Error: Should start from s[i-1]+1 to avoid printing left character again]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Should start from s[i-1]+1 to avoid printing left character again]

14.reason: // Loop prints right character when there are no intermediate characters (e.g., "0-1")

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Do-while loop will print the right character; should use while(c < s[i+1]) or correct initial value]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Loop prints right character when there are no intermediate characters (e.g., "0-1")
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The loop prints characters up to s[i+1], which will be printed again in the next iteration, causing duplication. The condition should be 'c < s[i+1] - 1'.

27.reason: // Loop prints right character when there are no intermediate characters (e.g., "a-b")

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Do-while loop will print the right character; should use while loop]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Loop prints right character when there are no intermediate characters (e.g., "a-b")
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Same duplication error: prints up to s[i+1], which is printed again next iteration. Condition should be 'c < s[i+1] - 1'.
